symptoms.symptom_name: Shortness of breath — cannot take a full breath, fast shallow breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Breathlessness with chest pain in a binder user is the classic presentation of pneumothorax; cannot be excluded without auscultation/imaging.
Tight thoracic compression restricts chest excursion, producing shallow tachypnoeic breathing.
Tachypnoea occurs in anxiety but cannot be assumed until organic causes are excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral costochondral / rib pain worse on inspiration, correlated with prolonged binding

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: mechanical chest-wall pain / costochondritis from chest binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral, garment-correlated, reproduced by compression and eased by loosening — mechanical pattern.
Sustained compression can injure ribs; needs palpation +/- imaging to distinguish.
Pleuritic-type pain must have pneumothorax excluded in person before a benign label is applied.

symptoms.symptom_name: Depersonalisation / feeling of floating outside the body

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: body image / gender-related distress with depersonalisation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute distress and body-focused anxiety can precipitate dissociative depersonalisation.
Non-specific; sealed clinician measurement. The AI infers 'racing heart' only from the patient's subjective report, not a measured rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oxygen saturation 97% and equal air entry bilaterally — clinician-measured/auscultated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Equal air entry and normal saturation lower pneumothorax probability — but this requires auscultation and a pulse oximeter the AI does not have, so it is sealed and cannot be relied on remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible costochondral tenderness on palpation with severe kyphosis / postural concealment

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: mechanical chest-wall pain / costochondritis from chest binding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable reproducible costochondral tenderness confirms a mechanical wall source — but palpation is impossible over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Restrictive eating with low body weight (~55 kg, poor appetite)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: body image / gender-related distress with disordered eating
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Restrictive eating and mirror avoidance cluster with body-image/gender distress and carry malnutrition and self-harm risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent low mood tied to body/identity (self-harm screen required)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Depressive affect linked to physical morphology is core to the psychosocial primary and drives the mandatory safety screen.

symptoms.symptom_name: No trauma / blow to chest; bilateral pain tracking garment use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of trauma and bilateral distribution make an acute traumatic fracture less likely, but compression injury is not excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden unilateral tearing pleuritic pain; no haemoptysis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lowers but does not exclude pneumothorax; definitive exclusion still needs in-person auscultation +/- imaging.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Breathlessness + pleuritic-type chest pain in a chest-binder user
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This pairing is the reason the case cannot be safely closed remotely — it mandates in-person exclusion regardless of the psychosocial context.
Frames the psychosocial primary and the mandatory mood/self-harm and eating screens.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person chest-wall examination + auscultation of breath sounds
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to assess equal air entry and reproducible tenderness — the core telehealth limitation; can only be done face-to-face.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray (if clinically indicated in person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude spontaneous pneumothorax or rib fracture when breathlessness and chest pain cannot be resolved on examination alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Mood / self-harm risk assessment and eating assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Body-image/gender distress with restrictive eating requires a safety and nutritional assessment as part of affirming care.
